up.units: mmol/gDCW/h (FBA reference/constraint flux used in all analyses)

growth_records.cite: Villada et al. Integrative Genome-Scale Metabolic Modeling Reveals Versatile Metabolic Strategies for Methane Utilization in Methylomicrobium album BG8. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00073-22. [PMC9040813]
growth_records.snippet: the growth kinetics of the cultures, assuming oxygen was the only limiting substrate, were determined by fitting a Monod kinetic model, resulting in a maximum growth rate (umax) of 0.11 h-1 (95% CI = 0.09 to 0.12) and a half-saturation cons
growth_records.notes: Batch culture, 30C, 200 rpm, NMS medium + 5 uM CuCl2; 20% CH4 / 20% O2 headspace (N2 balance). Reported max growth rate umax = 0.11 h-1 (Monod fit, O2 as sole limiting substrate; 95% CI 0.09-0.12), Km(O2) = 1.69 mM. Doubling time = ln2/umax = 0.6931/0.11 = 6.30 h (calculated). Growth rate per condition computed by authors as log-linear regression of biomass vs time over exponential phase (~16-40 h). Uptake/excretion fluxes computed by authors (mmol/gDCW/h) via regression of metabolite vs biomass divided by growth rate; per-condition numeric values appear only in figures (Fig 2E, 5D) and are not in text. Uptake rates listed here are the FBA reference constraints stated in text: methane fixed at 10 mmol/gDCW/h in all analyses; example O2 flux 15 mmol/gDCW/h at optimal 1.5:1 O2:CH4 ratio; O2 exchange lower limit -10 mmol/gDCW/h. O2:CH4 specific-uptake ratio ~1.5:1 optimal; 1.2-1.4:1 at 10-25% initial O2; ~0.5:1 at 5% O2. GAM 59.8 mmol ATP/gDCW, NGAM 3.5 mmol ATP/gDCW/h. Full NMS recipe cited (ref 72), not stated, so medium_composition_stated=false; pH not stated.
